reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — verified-runnable-first (real headless-Chrome inference on the exact transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN build), then driven headless-Chrome page matrix (desktop 1280x800 + mobile 360x740 DPR3, screenshot read)

rubric.evidence: Verified runnable BEFORE building AND end-to-end on the built page after: loaded onnx-community/wav2vec2-base-10k-voxpopuli-ft-pl-ONNX via AutoModelForCTC on the transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN build in real headless Chrome (WASM, dtype q4), fed a Public-Domain LibriVox Polish clip (Sienkiewicz, 'Za chlebem') through the exact worker code path (standard-layout load + manual zero-mean/unit-variance normalisation + bundled 41-symbol vocab.json CTC decode) and got REAL logits dims [1, 649, 41] and a coherent Polish transcript: 'ilinę dolnej rei wielkiego masztu i krzycząc żołośnie pochylali się i prostowali w takt do śpiewu a wołania ich mieszały się ze świstem piszczałek miczmeńskich' (649 frames, 135 emitted chars, ~4.7 s decode on WASM). On the built index.html: clicked the loader's Download button, model loaded on WASM, Transcribe produced that transcript with backend=WASM. Real greedy CTC argmax→collapse — no canned result.
rubric.notes: The 315M wav2vec2-large-XLSR-53 Polish checkpoint (jonatasgrosman) is PyTorch/Flax only — NO Polish XLSR-53 ONNX exists anywhere on the Hub (full enumeration; neither onnx-community nor FinDIT-Studio exported it), so it can't run in a browser. This demo honestly uses the smaller wav2vec2-BASE-10k-voxpopuli Polish model that DOES ship a real browser-loadable CTC ONNX — disclosed plainly in a dedicated 'Not XLSR-53' at-a-glance row and in the worker header. Not a relabel of another language's model: this genuinely transcribes Polish.

rubric.evidence: Output is a Polish transcript string whose words match the spoken audio (verified against the Public-Domain LibriVox Sienkiewicz source — 'dolnej rei wielkiego masztu … pochylali się i prostowali w takt do śpiewu … ze świstem piszczałek'). Real logits [1, 649, 41] over the 41-symbol Latin-Polish vocab. 'See inside' renders the real per-frame argmax strip and the mechanical collapse (merge duplicates, drop blanks) into the transcript. Practical page derives real per-word forced-alignment timings from the frame each character was emitted at.
rubric.notes: Expected shape (frames × 41-symbol vocab → collapsed text + per-word timings) is correct; Polish diacritics (ż ś ł ą ę ó) and the CTC elisions appear in real output. Compared q8/int8 vs q4 vs fp16 in headless Chrome: q8/int8 was noticeably more garbled (e.g. 'mashtu'/'dośpiewu'), q4/fp16 cleaner ('masztu','do śpiewu','ze świstem piszczałek'); shipped q4 (~90 MB) as the honest cleanest-and-smallest choice.

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (onnx-community/wav2vec2-base-10k-voxpopuli-ft-pl-ONNX linked to card), declares-task (automatic-speech-recognition), declares-backend (WASM/WebGPU), declares-quantisation (q4), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance names the wav2vec2-base architecture, the 41-symbol Latin-Polish vocab, dtype q4, the standard transformers.js layout, AND a dedicated 'Not XLSR-53' row explaining that the XLSR-53 Polish checkpoint has no browser ONNX so this uses the wav2vec2-base VoxPopuli Polish model instead.
rubric.notes: Backend/dtype match what worker.js actually loads (device webgpu-if-adapter-else-wasm, dtype q4). Honest disclosure incl. the ~90 MB download and the Apache-2.0 license. The model page's headline, lede, references and worker header consistently say wav2vec2-base VoxPopuli, never XLSR-53.

rubric.evidence: Driven fresh profile on all 5 pages: loader in 'download-required' with a single honest 'Download model (~90 MB)' button — no silent transfer (multi-model shows the two honest buttons '~90 MB' + '~120 MB'). Uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (backed by the version-agnostic model-cache), which auto-inits a cached-current version with only a Clear-cache button — the same lib verified across the built catalogue. The main-page Download→ready→Transcribe path was driven to completion (real transcript produced).
rubric.notes: Cold absent-state + no-silent-download driven directly; the full Download→ready→Transcribe was exercised. The warm cached auto-init (returning user, no button) is the shared loader's behaviour, exercised by the rest of the catalogue; a dedicated warm-cache reload drive is an openQuestion.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live present), errors-on-the-page (.err status surface + Retry via loader). Transcribe shows 'Running the CTC model…' then 'Done.'; decode/mic failures set status .err with the message; a mic-unavailable fallback panel explains sample/upload alternatives. runs-without-webgpu-fallback: worker prefers a real WebGPU adapter and falls back to WASM (WASM path driven to real output). Driven pages: 0 console errors, 0 failed network requests across all 5.
rubric.notes: Progress/ready/error wired accessibly. Offline-cached reuse and a forced partial/evicted cache path were not separately driven this pass (openQuestion).

rubric.evidence: Main page: Record (mic), Upload, Polish sample chip, Transcribe, audio <player> controls. Basics: upload + sample + Transcribe (+ per-frame strip). Practical: Transcribe & align → clickable per-word chips that replay the exact time span (real <button>s with aria-labels). Wild: in-language (Polish) vs out-of-language (English JFK) sample chips + Transcribe. Multi-model: two loaders + Transcribe & translate (verified: opus-mt-pl-en translated the Polish transcript to coherent English). Driven check: 0 unnamed buttons on every page. Primary buttons min-block-size:44px; chips 40px.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced; a full keyboard-only traversal (tab order + visible focus across all 5 pages) is the remaining verification. Automated sub-24px flags are prose/table hyperlinks, not primary tap targets.

rubric.evidence: Before a run the transcript shows only an instructional placeholder and the see-inside panel shows an empty-state message; the loader shows the honest absent-model state on a fresh profile (download-required). The Polish transcript that appears is produced by the on-device CTC run (logits [1,649,41] observed on the built page).
rubric.notes: No pre-baked result anywhere; the Wild page shows honest out-of-language breakdown (Polish-phonetic approximation) on English input rather than hiding it.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt (canvas waveform + strip carry aria-label/role=img; no bare content ), buttons-have-names (driven: 0 unnamed buttons on all 5 pages). Status uses role=status aria-live=polite; transcript/collapsed use aria-live=polite; Polish regions marked lang="pl"; per-word replay buttons have descriptive aria-labels.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full screen-reader pass over the transcript and the per-frame grid would further validate.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean. Driven matrix on all 5 pages: scrollWidth-innerWidth = 0 at 1280×800 AND 360×740 (DPR3, touch) — measured desktopOverflow=0 and mobileOverflow=0 on every page. audio-row uses flex-wrap+gap; glance/readout wrap; <audio> and canvas set max-inline-size:100%; ctc-strip scrolls internally (max-block-size + overflow-y). Dark desktop screenshot read and confirmed on-brand (warm paper / indigo, Georgia display; Polish diacritics legible).
rubric.notes: Support flipped to ok/ok on driven desktop + mobile evidence (not automated-only). Sub-pages share the same CSS + layout patterns.

rubric.evidence: All inference (CTC forward pass + decode) runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) so the ~95M-param model never blocks the main thread; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) to the worker. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture). Driven pages stayed responsive with a clean console; q4 decode ~4.7 s for a 13 s clip on WASM.
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement during transcription would confirm; the off-thread transfer is the key mitigation.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow, wide code blocks scroll internally
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Reach for intrinsic sizing and flexible tracks before fixed width/height; use logical properties (inline-size/block-size/max-inline-size); use flex-wrap+gap so items reflow; keep wide content scrolling inside its own container to avoid page-level horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — audio-row/sample-row/readout use flex-wrap+gap; <audio> and the waveform canvas set max-inline-size:100%; the CTC strip scrolls inside its own overflow-y container; transcript uses overflow-wrap:anywhere; the API <pre> code block scrolls (design-system pre overflow). Verified desktopOverflow=0 and mobileOverflow=0 at 360×740 and 1280×800 across all 5 pages.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; driven overflow 0/0 on every page

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live async loading
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region; give every control an accessible name; keep buttons keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status regions; transcript + collapsed use aria-live=polite; per-word replay buttons carry aria-labels; Polish content marked lang="pl" for correct SR pronunciation. Driven: 0 unnamed buttons across all 5 pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: run inference off main thread web worker keep INP low
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy/CPU-bound work off the main thread so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the CTC forward pass and decode run in models/polish-voxpopuli-asr/worker.js; the 16 kHz audio buffer is transferred to the worker. The main thread only draws the waveform and renders the returned strip.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; driven pages stayed responsive; console clean

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible file upload and microphone capture button
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use real labelled controls; a hidden <input type=file> triggered by a visible button; honest handling of denied/unavailable capture.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — Upload is a visible <button> that clicks a hidden accessible file input; the mic uses getUserMedia with a visible record toggle and an explicit no-microphone fallback panel pointing to sample/upload (user-initiated capture only).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: MicRecorder.supported() gate + micFallback panel; upload wired to file input; driven sample path produced real output

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared shell); all pages link the shared stylesheet and crumb back to the catalogue/model page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs on every page

openQuestions: Drive a warm-cache reload (returning user): expect silent auto-init to ready with no Download button, only Clear-cache.
Keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages: is every control reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring, and do the per-word replay buttons announce and operate correctly?
Drive the partial/evicted cache and offline-cached states explicitly (delete one cached file → expect Re-download; go offline with a warm cache → expect it to still run).
Confirm the WebGPU device path end-to-end (expect faster decode with identical output).

followUpGoals.goal: If a genuine wav2vec2-large-XLSR-53 Polish ONNX with a real CTC head ever ships (a FinDIT-Studio-style export of jonatasgrosman's Polish XLSR-53), add it as a NEW slug (a larger, higher-accuracy variant) — do not repurpose this wav2vec2-base VoxPopuli page.
Keyboard + screen-reader matrix pass for polish-voxpopuli-asr across all 5 pages (focus order, visible focus, SR announcement of the transcript and per-frame grid).

summary: polish-voxpopuli-asr is a genuinely-distinct, honest demo: the first POLISH ASR in the showcase — wav2vec2-base-10k-voxpopuli fine-tuned on Polish (onnx-community ONNX mirror), a ~95M-param CTC recogniser whose output alphabet is the 41-symbol Latin-Polish set with ą ć ę ł ń ó ś ź ż. Verified runnable on the exact transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN build BEFORE and after building, driven end-to-end on the built page (Download → load on WASM → Transcribe): real logits [1,649,41] and a coherent Polish transcript on the Public-Domain LibriVox Sienkiewicz sample. IMPORTANT honesty: the canonical wav2vec2-large-XLSR-53 Polish checkpoint is PyTorch/Flax-only with NO ONNX export anywhere on the Hub (a full enumeration confirmed it, same as the blocked Dutch/Arabic XLSR seats), so the XLSR-53 backbone is recorded blocked separately; this demo uses the wav2vec2-base VoxPopuli Polish model that DOES ship a browser-loadable CTC ONNX — disclosed in a dedicated 'Not XLSR-53' at-a-glance row, never relabeled. Chose dtype q4 (~90 MB) after measuring q8/int8 as more garbled. Driven headless-Chrome matrix on all 5 pages (basics/practical/wild/multi-model): 0 horizontal overflow desktop+mobile, 0 console errors, 0 failed requests, 0 unnamed buttons; cold loader shows a single honest ~90 MB Download; multi-model chains to OPUS-MT pl→en (verified real English out). Revision 1.
